content.text:
I attempted to locate a node named exactly "Microsoft" under Cornerstone, but I could not find any matching nodes in the mesh at the searched locations.

Possible reasons:
- The Cornerstone node or the Microsoft child does not exist at the expected path in the mesh.
- The Microsoft node uses a different exact name or is nested under a different parent.
- I may not have permission or the graph context needed to see those nodes.

Please choose one of the options below so I can proceed:

1) Provide the exact mesh path to the Microsoft node (for example: @Cornerstone/Microsoft or host/123/data/Cornerstone/Microsoft). I’ll use that to locate and duplicate it.

2) Allow me to search the entire mesh for a node named "Microsoft" (not limited to directly under Cornerstone). If you permit that, I’ll search and report matches for you to choose from.

3) If Cornerstone exists but I don’t have visibility, grant or confirm the correct address/context to search (or tell me where Cornerstone is stored in the mesh).

4) If the Microsoft node is not present and you want me to create a Nestle node from a Microsoft template you can upload or point to, provide that template or confirm you want me to create a new pricing node from scratch with default placeholders.

When you reply, also confirm whether I should:
- Proceed automatically if exactly one suitable Microsoft node is found, or
- Pause and ask you to select if multiple suitable Microsoft nodes are found.

Once you provide the path or permission, I will locate the Microsoft node and report: node path, nodeType, and whether it has children/content/layout areas. Then I will proceed with duplication only after your confirmation.
